content_markdown:
The average surface temperature of Mars is about $200\text{ K}$. The average surface temperature of Earth is about $300\text{ K}$. Both can be regarded as black bodies.

What is the ratio $\frac{\text{energy radiated per second per unit area on Mars}}{\text{energy radiated per second per unit area on Earth}}$?
vibe_explanation: According to the Stefan-Boltzmann law, the power radiated per unit area (intensity) of a black body is proportional to the fourth power of its absolute temperature ($P/A = \sigma T^4$). Therefore, the ratio of the energy radiated per second per unit area is $(\frac{T_{\text{Mars}}}{T_{\text{Earth}}})^4 = (\frac{200}{300})^4 = (\frac{2}{3})^4 = \frac{16}{81} \approx 0.1975$, which rounds to 0.2.
